name: Jan
tagline: Run open-source LLMs offline on your own computer, like a private ChatGPT
seoTitle: Jan - Open-Source Offline ChatGPT for Local LLMs
metaDescription: Jan is an open-source desktop app that downloads and runs LLMs fully offline, with an OpenAI-compatible local API and optional cloud model connections.
overview: Jan is an open-source desktop app that lets you download and run large language models directly on your own machine. It works like a ChatGPT-style assistant, but the models can run fully offline, so your chats and data stay on your computer.

It is aimed at developers and privacy-minded users who want local control over which models they run. You can pull open models such as Llama, Gemma, Qwen, and GPT-oss from Hugging Face, or connect to cloud providers like OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral, and Groq when you prefer.

As a local runtime, Jan also exposes an OpenAI-compatible API server at localhost:1337, so other applications can talk to your local models using the same request format they already use for hosted APIs.
features: Download and run open LLMs (Llama, Gemma, Qwen, GPT-oss) from Hugging Face
OpenAI-compatible local API server at localhost:1337 for other apps
Optional cloud model connections: OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral, Groq, MiniMax, and others
Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration for agentic workflows
Runs locally for privacy; installers for Windows, macOS, and Linux

gettingStarted.intro: The quickest way to start is to download the prebuilt desktop app for your operating system; you can also build from source if you prefer.

steps.heading: Download the app
steps.body: Get the installer for your platform from jan.ai or GitHub Releases (Windows .exe, macOS .dmg, or Linux .deb/.AppImage), then install and open it.

steps.heading: Download a model and chat
steps.body: Inside the app, download an open model such as Llama, Gemma, or Qwen from Hugging Face, then start chatting with it locally. No code required.

steps.heading: Use the local API (optional)
steps.body: Jan exposes an OpenAI-compatible server at localhost:1337, so existing OpenAI-style clients can point at your local models.

steps.heading: Build from source (optional)
steps.body: Requires Node.js >= 20, Yarn >= 4.5.3, Make, and Rust (for Tauri). The dev target installs dependencies, builds, and launches the app.
steps.code:
git clone https://github.com/janhq/jan
cd jan
make dev

useCases: Run a private, offline AI assistant without sending chats to a cloud service
Test and compare open models like Llama, Gemma, and Qwen on your own hardware
Serve a local OpenAI-compatible endpoint for your own apps and scripts
Switch between local models and cloud providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Groq) from one app
